inputs.prompt: Station: Metro FM, daytime news. Duration: 3 minutes. Stories: 1) City hospital declares critical incident after surge in emergency admissions — 14-hour waits reported, hospital trust CEO says staffing levels are at breaking point. 2) National education union announces one-day strike for next Thursday over pay and workload — affecting primary and secondary schools. 3) Local football club announces new community stadium project — £45M investment, 15,000 capacity, to open 2028. Include clip: Hospital CEO (10 secs) — out-cue: '...and we cannot continue to operate safely at these levels.'
Station: Campus Radio. Duration: 2 minutes. Stories: 1) University announces tuition fee increase of 8% next year. 2) Student union elections next week — three candidates for president.

Station: European English-language radio network. Duration: 3 minutes. Stories: 1) EU Parliament votes to impose tariffs on imported steel from three non-EU countries — trade unions welcome the move, manufacturers warn of higher costs. 2) Flooding in southern Germany causes evacuation of 2,000 residents — three people missing, rescue operations ongoing. 3) European Central Bank holds interest rates steady for the fourth consecutive meeting — ECB president says inflation is 'within striking distance' of target. Include clip: ECB President (12 secs) — out-cue: '...and we see no reason to adjust our position at this time.'
